project: ArcSafe — N-of-M multi-signature wallet for Arc Chain

findings.title: Deployer private key stored in cleartext in .env
findings.root_cause: Deployer secret kept unencrypted at rest. Not committed (gitignored; tree not under git), but a cleartext copy on disk is exposed by any zip/backup/screen-share or a premature git init.
findings.impact: Full control of every account the key owns. Bounded to testnet today; becomes Critical and an irreversible loss of funds if reused for a mainnet deployer or an owner of a funded safe.
findings.remediation: Treat as testnet-only/disposable; never reuse for mainnet or a funded owner slot; rotate if ever shared/backed up/committed; prefer an encrypted keystore or secret manager over a plaintext file; verify .gitignore covers .env before any git init.
findings.remediation_applied: Full-tree scan confirmed the key appears only in .env (other 64-hex hits are tx hashes/bytecode). Added scripts/check-secrets.js + `npm run check:secrets` guard. Key value unchanged — rotation is owner-only and remains the open item.
findings.validation_test: npm run check:secrets => OK; grep -nE '0x[0-9a-fA-F]{64}' .env matches only a confirmed throwaway.

findings.title: No Content-Security-Policy or security response headers; site served over plain HTTP
findings.root_cause: Security headers for a static export are set at the web server; none were configured in-repo or documented for the nginx deployment, and the live site was served over HTTP.
findings.impact: Reduced defense-in-depth for a high-value wallet dApp: clickjacking via missing frame-ancestors, in-transit tampering without HTTPS/HSTS. Injected-script XSS surface is low (React auto-escapes, no innerHTML/eval, no cookies), hence Medium not High.
findings.remediation: Serve over HTTPS and add CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy at nginx; self-host fonts to tighten CSP.
findings.remediation_applied: Added deploy/nginx-arcsafe.conf (HTTPS server block with CSP scoped to the two Arc RPC origins, HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY, nosniff, Referrer-Policy no-referrer, Permissions-Policy, COOP, HTTP->HTTPS redirect, immutable asset caching). Added <meta name=referrer content=no-referrer> in _app.tsx. Effective once deployed over TLS.

findings.title: Fund-custody contract is unaudited by a third party
findings.root_cause: Pre-audit software, openly disclosed in README and the app UI.
findings.impact: Bounded to testnet now; roadmap items (EIP-712 approvals, modules, session keys, ERC-4337, mainnet) materially raise the value of an independent audit before shipping.
findings.remediation: Keep the not-audited/testnet disclosure prominent; commission an independent audit before mainnet or before adding off-chain signature approvals; re-run this pass after each roadmap feature.
findings.validation_test: Process: audit report on file before a mainnet deploy tag.

findings.title: Unreachable SafeAlreadyExists error yields opaque CREATE2 collision revert
findings.root_cause: Dead error declared but never reverted; a same-salt/same-args createSafe reverted at the EVM level from the new ArcSafe{salt} collision with no decodable custom error.
findings.impact: No fund/authorization impact; a colliding deployment surfaced as a generic revert the frontend could not humanize.
findings.remediation: Pre-check predicted CREATE2 address and revert SafeAlreadyExists() when occupied; map the error in the UI.
findings.remediation_applied: createSafe now pre-checks via a shared _predict helper (refactored out of predictAddress) and reverts SafeAlreadyExists(). Error added to FACTORY_ABI and humanizeError. New regression test added. Suite 43 passing. Requires a factory redeploy to carry the new bytecode on-chain.
findings.validation_test: Hardhat test 'rejects a repeat deployment with a named error instead of an opaque revert' => passing.

false_positives: window.location.reload() in wallet.ts chainChanged handler — cache invalidation on network change, not a redirect/injection sink.
http:// matches — only in Markdown docs; all runtime RPC/explorer URLs are HTTPS (the plain-HTTP serving of the site is tracked separately as SEC-002).
NEXT_PUBLIC_* env values — intended public contract addresses, never secrets; no key is NEXT_PUBLIC_.
64-hex matches in artifacts/ and docs — transaction hashes and contract bytecode, not the private key (confirmed the literal key value appears only in .env).
patch_note: Working tree is not under git, so security-fixes.patch is a documented file-by-file change list rather than a git diff. No secret value appears in it.
